surface_verification.justification: Framework-internal Claude-Code hook output-channel fix (PostToolUse security guards check_secrets.sh + check_destructive_migration.sh; SessionStart artifact-drift detector). No web/CLI/API product surface exists for this change. Behavior is verified end-to-end by shared/tests/test_hook_block_channel.py, which subprocesses the REAL registered hook commands with production-shaped stdin payloads and asserts the reason lands on the channel each event actually reads.

behaviors.behavior: PostToolUse check_secrets.sh: on a detected secret -> exit 2 with the block reason on stderr (the channel Claude reads on exit 2), NOT on the discarded stdout
PostToolUse check_destructive_migration.sh (registered build copy): on destructive SQL -> exit 2 with the reason on stderr, not stdout

SessionStart artifact-drift gate (check_artifact_drift.py -> stale_artifact_detector.hook_main): a migrated/block finding is delivered to the model via a schema-valid additionalContext payload on stdout + stderr notice + exit 0 (warn-only; SessionStart cannot block) — the hook<->detector composition exercised end-to-end via subprocess of the real registered hook
SessionStart drift gate fail-open: a report-write OSError must NOT suppress the warning — still emit additionalContext + exit 0
in_progress (warn-severity) drift path: stderr notice + report + exit 0 (unchanged by this diff)
behaviors.reason: behavior is unchanged by this diff; pinned by the pre-existing test_stale_artifact_detector.py::test_hook_main_returns_zero_with_warn_only
